title: Blackboard
description: Schema defining configuration properties for the crospi_node

general.title: General parameters
general.description: General parameters to be used to configure the ROS node.

sample_time.description: At each sample_time in seconds, eTaSL computes new robot joint velocities

event_topic.description: Name of the topic in which string events are going to be published (e.g. when a task finishes)

initializer.title: Initialization parameters
initializer.description: Parameters to be used in the initialization phase of eTaSL

full.description: full initialization for constraints of all priorities

sample_time.description: sample time for the simulation during initialization

duration.description: duration of the simulation during initialization

convergence_criterion.description: stop the simulation during initialization of this criterion is reached

weightfactor.dscription: weightfactor used to deal with the multiple priorities during initialization

oneOf.title: QPOases Parameters
oneOf.description: eTaSL constraints are translated into a quadratic problem.  This specifies that the QPOases solver will be used to solve this numeric problem

is-qpoasessolver.description: key to indicate the QPOases solver will be used (boolean, but value is of no importance)

nWSR.description: maximum number of working set recalcuations to be performed during initial homiotopy

regularization_factor.description: regularization factor used in the transcription to a numeric problem.  A higher value lowers joint velocities in the neighbourhoud of singularities, but decreases the adherence to the soft constraints

cputime.description: maximum allowed CPU time in seconds for the initialisation of QPOases

robot.title: eTaSL robot configuration
robot.description: Configuration to setup the robot kinematic model and robot driver

robotsimulators.title: RobotSimulators
robotsimulators.description: A RobotSimulator interfaces simulated robot hardware with eTaSL and runs in a separate thread (one per each RobotSimulator), communicating via shared memory (lock-free and wait-free) with the eTaSL thread for reduced latency.

items.title: Robotsimulator
items.description: RobotSimulator interfaces simulated robot hardware with eTaSL and runs in a separate thread, communicating with shared memory for reduced latency.

is-simple_kinematic_simulation.description: To indicate that the task will be executed in simulation, with a virtual robot that integrates joint velocities and gives back joint positions to eTaSL.

robot_joints.description: Names of the joints that will be simulated through this robotsimulator. This names need to be consistent with the ones declared in the URDF file of the robot and must exist also in the robot_joints declared in the default_robot_specification.

initial_joints.description: Initial joint values in [rad] for rotational joints or [m] for prismatic joints. The size must coincide with the length of the robot_joints of the specified robot.

periodicity.description: Periodicity in which the integration will be performed in seconds. 1/periodicity is the update frequency in Hz.

name_expr_joint_vel.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Defines the name of the expression that will be used to put the joint velocities coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name_0 = ctx:createInputChannelScalar('selected_name_0') for each joint

name_expr_joint_torque.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Defines the name of the expression that will be used to put the joint torques coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name_0 = ctx:createInputChannelScalar('selected_name_0') for each joint

name_expr_joint_current.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Defines the name of the expression that will be used to put the joint currents coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name_0 = ctx:createInputChannelScalar('selected_name_0') for each joint

name_expr_cartesian_pos.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the cartesian positions coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelVector('selected_name')

name_expr_cartesian_quat.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the cartesian orientation coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelRotation('selected_name')

name_expr_cartesian_twist.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the cartesian twist coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelTwist('selected_name')

name_expr_cartesian_wrench.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the cartesian wrench coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelWrench('selected_name')

name_expr_base_pos.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the position of the base of the robot coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelVector('selected_name')

name_expr_base_quat.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the orientation of the base of the robot coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelRotation('selected_name')

name_expr_base_twist.description: If defined, the simulator will generate a ramp. Name of the expression that will be used to put the twist of the base of the robot coming from this simulator. This name can be then used in etasl with selected_name = ctx:createInputChannelTwist('selected_name')

robotdrivers.title: Robotdrivers
robotdrivers.description: A Robotdriver interfaces robot hardware with eTaSL and runs in a separate thread (one per Robotdriver), communicating via shared memory (lock-free and wait-free) with the eTaSL thread for reduced latency

items.title: Robotdriver
items.description: Robotdriver interfaces robot hardware with eTaSL and runs in a separate thread, communicating via shared memory for reduced latency

is-template_driver_crospi.description: To indicate that the tasks will be executed using the template_driver_crospi driver when running on the real robot.

robot_joints.description: Names of the joints that will be interfaced through this robot driver. This names need to be consistent with the ones declared in the URDF file of the robot and must exist also in the robot_joints declared in the default_robot_specification.

initial_joints.description: Initial joint values in [rad] for rotational joints or [m] for prismatic joints. The size must coincide with the length of the robot_joints of the specified robot.

periodicity.description: Periodicity in which the integration will be performed in seconds. 1/periodicity is the update frequency in Hz.

is-inline_robotspecification.description: To indicate that the robot specification will be declared inline within the JSON configuration file (and not in an external lua file).

urdf_path.description: Path of the urdf. Use $[colcon_package] to make the path relative to a colcon package

robot_joints.description: Names of the joints in order from the world to the end effector. This names need to be consistent with the ones declared in the URDF file of the robot.

tcp_frame.description: Tool center point frame that goes from parent_link to the child_link. A variable with the name tcp_frame will be available in each LUA task specification.

parent_link.description: Name of the frame parent_link (i.e. base link), which should exist as a link in the URDF.

child_link.description: Name of the frame child_link (i.e. link of interest), which should exist as a link in the URDF.

additional_frames.title: additional_frames
additional_frames.description: List of additional frames that are declared with the purpose of imposing constraints over them in the task specification

items.description: Additional frame that is declared with the purpose of imposing constraints over it in the task specification

name.description: Name of the frame that goes from parent_link to the child_link. A variable will be created in LUA with this name and will be available in each task specification.

parent_link.description: Name of the frame parent_link (i.e. base link), which should exist as a link in the URDF.

child_link.description: Name of the frame child_link (i.e. link of interest), which should exist as a link in the URDF.

is-lua_robotspecification.description: To indicate that the robot specification will be declared in a lua file, which enables flexibility for more advanced applications.

file_path.description: Path of the LUA file containing the robot specification. Start with $[colcon_package] to make the path relative to a colcon_package, or start with / to make it an absolute path.

robot_joints.description: Names of the joints in order from the world to the end effector. This names need to be consistent with the ones declared in the URDF file of the robot.

iohandlers.title: IO Handler configuration
iohandlers.description: Configuration to setup input and output handlers to the etasl node (e.g. input/output ROS2 topics)

outputhandlers.title: Outputhandlers
outputhandlers.description: Outputhandlers extract data from eTaSL and communicate it in some way to the outside world, such as writing to a file or using a ROS Topic

items.title: Outputhandler
items.description: An outputhandler extract data from eTaSL and communicate this in some way to the outside world, e.g. writing to a ROS2 Topic or other types of communication

oneOf.title: JointStateOutputHandler
oneOf.description: An outputhandler that outputs the joint state, i.e. position and values of the robot variables

is-JointStateOutputHandler.description: A key value to flag that this describes a JointStateOutputHandler

topic-name.description: Name of the ROS topic that will serve as output

oneOf.title: TopicOutputHandler
oneOf.description: An outputhandler that outputs to a topic scalar values defined as Output Expressions in eTaSL.

is-TopicOutputHandler.description: A key to indicate that this describes a TopicOutputHandler which publishes scalar values defined as Output Expressions in eTaSL.

topic-name.description: Name of the ROS topic that will serve as output.

variable-names.description:
List of names of (scalar!) output expressions to output. 
 The current scalar value and a boolean is_declared are published for each of these variables to indicate if the variable is declared as a scalar output expression within the currently running eTaSL task specification.

oneOf.title: TopicOutputDerivativesHandler
oneOf.description: An outputhandler that outputs the derivatives of variables/expressions to a topic scalar values defined as Output Expressions in eTaSL.

is-TopicOutputDerivativesHandler.description: A key to indicate that this describes a TopicOutputDerivativesHandler which publishes scalar values defined as Output Expressions in eTaSL.

topic-name.description: Name of the ROS topic that will serve as output.

variable-names.description:
List of names of (scalar!) output expressions to output. 
 The current scalar value and a boolean is_declared are published for each of these variables to indicate if the variable is declared as a scalar output expression within the currently running eTaSL task specification.

inputhandlers.title: Inputhandlers
inputhandlers.description: Inputhandlers get information from the outside world and puts it into eTaSL

items.title: Inputhandler
items.description: An inputhandler get information from the outside world and put it into eTaSL, e.g. via ROS2 topics or other types of communication

is-TwistInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a twist inputhandler which reads from a geometry_msgs/Twist Message topic and converts it into a twist etasl expression

topic-name.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

depth.description: Depth of the topic queue (i.e. buffer size storing incoming messages). Check ROS2 QoS policies for more information

linear.title: Screw Linear
linear.description: Linear term (i.e. angular velocity or torque) of a screw (i.e. twist or wrench) vector

angular.title: Screw Angular
angular.description: Angular term (i.e. angular velocity or torque) of a screw (i.e. twist or wrench) vector

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_twist field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

varname.description: Name of the expression variable that was created in the task specification with createInputChannelTwist. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through this variable

is-WrenchInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a wrench inputhandler which reads from a geometry_msgs/Wrench Message topic and converts it into a wrench etasl expression

topic-name.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

depth.description: Depth of the topic queue (i.e. buffer size storing incoming messages). Check ROS2 QoS policies for more information

force.title: Screw Linear
force.description: Linear term (i.e. angular velocity or torque) of a screw (i.e. twist or wrench) vector

torque.title: Screw Angular
torque.description: Angular term (i.e. angular velocity or torque) of a screw (i.e. twist or wrench) vector

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_wrench field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

varname.description: Name of the expression variable that was created in the task specification with createInputChannelWrench. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through this variable

oneOf.title: TopicInputHandler
oneOf.description: An input handler that reads from a topic

is-TopicInputHandler.description: A key to indicate that this describes a TopicInputHandler

topic-name.description: Name of the ROS topic that will serve as input

is-PoseInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a Pose inputhandler which reads a Pose from a topic and converts it into a frame etasl expression

topic-name.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

depth.description: Depth of the topic queue (i.e. buffer size storing incoming messages). Check ROS2 QoS policies for more information

feedforward_input_topic.description: (optional) If set, the inputhandler will read from the specified topic of type geometry_msgs/Twist and will be used as feedforward (i.e. time derivative of the pose)

orientation.title: orientation
orientation.description: orientation of frame given my normalized quaternion

oneOf.title: Normalized quaternion
oneOf.description: orientation of frame given my normalized quaternion

oneOf.title: Normalized orientation
oneOf.description: orientation of frame given by roll, pitch, yaw angles

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_pose field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

varname.description: Name of the expression variable in the task specification with createInputChannelFrame. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through this variable

is-VectorInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a Vector inputhandler which reads a Vector3 or a Point message type from a topic and converts it into a vector etasl expression

topic-name.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

msg_type.description: name of the topic to subscribe to

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

depth.description: Depth of the topic queue (i.e. buffer size storing incoming messages). Check ROS2 QoS policies for more information

feedforward_input_topic.description: (optional). Only use if the vector is a position (and not a velocity) vector. If set, the inputhandler will read from the specified topic of type geometry_msgs/Vector and will be used as feedforward (i.e. time derivative of the vector, if the vector is at a position level)

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_vector field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

varname.description: Name of the expression variable in the task specification with createInputChannelFrame. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through this variable

is-TFInputHandler.description: To indicate that this describes a TF inputhandler which reads a TF from a source frame to a target frame and converts it into a frame etasl expression

number_of_tries.description: the number of times unsuccesfully initialize() can be called before throwing an error

feedforward_input_topic.description: (optional) If set, the inputhandler will read from the specified topic of type geometry_msgs/Twist and will be used as feedforward (i.e. time derivative of the frame generated from the TF transform)

orientation.title: orientation
orientation.description: orientation of frame given my normalized quaternion

oneOf.title: Normalized quaternion
oneOf.description: orientation of frame given my normalized quaternion

oneOf.title: Normalized orientation
oneOf.description: orientation of frame given by roll, pitch, yaw angles

when_unpublished.description: Defines the behavior of the node when nothing has been published to the topic defined in topic-name during activation time of the node

oneOf.title: Use default value
oneOf.description: Uses the defined default value in the default_pose field while nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name. An INFO message will be logged.

oneOf.title: Throw error
oneOf.description: Throws an error if nothing has been published to the subscribed topic defined in topic-name at the time of activation of the node, preventing its activation.

child_link.description: Name of the child link whose frame will be used for the requested transformation (in TF notation is called target frame, since the equivalent transformation would transform the data TO this frame)

parent_link.description: Name of the parent link whose frame will be used for the requested transformation (in TF notation is called source frame, since the equivalent transformation would transform the data FROM this frame)

varname.description: Name of the expression variable in the task specification with createInputChannelFrame. The data coming from the topic will be available in the task specification through this variable
